mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) or near-zero monetary accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity valuations contract due to lower expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth simultaneously, aggregate demand expansion drives corporate profit expectations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move in the same direction to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344649M and HOUST at 550K units
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and shift to defensive positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: supply shock or stagflation risk environment
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals labor market health and consumer income expansion. When PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month, this indicates robust job creation that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market expansion or recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real interest rates. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized and FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, real rates are deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate signals aggregate demand trajectory. When GDPC1 shows quarter-over-quarter growth acceleration (positive second derivative), equity investors increase earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days. This is an always-on mechanism that operates whenever GDP growth is accelerating.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of periods with GDP acceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) diverge when consumer confidence fragments. When HOUST declines while RSAFS remains flat or grows, this signals a shift from durable goods demand to services consumption, indicating demand reallocation rather than expansion. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in late-cycle environments where such divergence signals demand exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 2% month-over-month while RSAFS growth < 0.5% month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand composition shift
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of co-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient reverses sign (becomes positive) in rolling 2-year windows.
